name: Place Order
summary: Places a new order to the OMS via a POST call. The request carries a jData JSON object (order details) plus the session jKey; on success it returns the assigned Noren order number (norenordno).

request_params.name: jData
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in the json_fields list below.

json_fields.name: uid
json_fields.description: Logged in User Id.

json_fields.name: actid
json_fields.description: Login users account ID.

json_fields.name: exch
json_fields.description: Exchange (Select from 'exarr' Array provided in User Details response).

json_fields.name: tsym
json_fields.description: Unique id of contract on which order to be placed. (use url encoding to avoid special char error for symbols like M&M)

json_fields.name: qty
json_fields.description: Order Quantity [If qty is junk value other than numbers].

json_fields.name: prc
json_fields.description: Order Price [If prc is junk value other than numbers] "Order price cannot be zero".

json_fields.name: trgprc
json_fields.description: Only to be sent in case of SL / SL-M order.

json_fields.name: dscqty
json_fields.description: Disclosed quantity (Max 10% for NSE, and 50% for MCX) [If dscqty is junk value other than numbers].

json_fields.name: prd
json_fields.description: Product name (Select from 'prarr' Array provided in User Details response, and if same is allowed for selected, exchange. Show product display name, for user to select, and send corresponding prd in API call).
json_fields.possible_values: C - CNC / M - NRML / H - CO / B - BO / I - MIS / F - MTF

json_fields.name: trantype
json_fields.description: B -> BUY, S -> SELL [transtype should be 'B' or 'S' else reject].

json_fields.name: prctyp
json_fields.description: Price type.

json_fields.name: ret
json_fields.description: Retention type [ret should be DAY / EOS / IOC else reject].

json_fields.name: remarks
json_fields.description: Any tag by user to mark order.

json_fields.name: ordersource
json_fields.description: Used to generate exchange info fields.

json_fields.name: bpprc
json_fields.description: Book Profit Price applicable only if product is selected as B (Bracket order).

json_fields.name: blprc
json_fields.description: Book loss Price applicable only if product is selected as H and B (High Leverage and Bracket order).

json_fields.name: trailprc
json_fields.description: Trailing Price applicable only if product is selected as H and B (High Leverage and Bracket order).

json_fields.name: amo
json_fields.description: The message "Invalid AMO" will be displayed if the "amo" field is not sent with a "Yes" value. If amo is not required, do not send this field.

json_fields.name: tsym2
json_fields.description: Trading symbol of second leg, mandatory for price type 2L and 3L (use url encoding to avoid special char error for symbols like M&M).

json_fields.name: trantype2
json_fields.description: Transaction type of second leg, mandatory for price type 2L and 3L.

json_fields.name: qty2
json_fields.description: Quantity for second leg, mandatory for price type 2L and 3L.

json_fields.name: prc2
json_fields.description: Price for second leg, mandatory for price type 2L and 3L.

json_fields.name: tsym3
json_fields.description: Trading symbol of third leg, mandatory for price type 3L (use url encoding to avoid special char error for symbols like M&M).

json_fields.name: trantype3
json_fields.description: Transaction type of third leg, mandatory for price type 3L.

json_fields.name: qty3
json_fields.description: Quantity for third leg, mandatory for price type 3L.

json_fields.name: prc3
json_fields.description: Price for third leg, mandatory for price type 3L.

response_fields.name: stat
response_fields.description: Place order success or failure indication.

response_fields.name: norenordno
response_fields.description: It will be present only on successful Order placement to OMS.

issues: Removed an 'emsg' RESPONSE field that was in the draft but is NOT in the RESPONSE DETAILS table on page 9 (which lists only stat / request_time / norenordno); emsg appears only in the sample failure response, not as a documented response field. The cleaned text flattens the table and splits multi-line possible-value cells (e.g. prd's 'C - CNC / M - NRML / H - CO / B - BO / I - MIS / F - MTF' and exch's 'NSE / NFO / BSE / MCX') across several lines; these were rebound to the correct fields using the page images. ordersource carries possible value 'API' but has no trailing '*', so it is recorded as optional. Apparent source inconsistency: amo carries a trailing '*' (marked mandatory) yet its own description and the sample curl say not to send it when not an AMO, and the curl omits it.
notes:
- BaseUrl in samples = https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI ; ClientId example FT0000 ; jKey example GHUDWU53H32MTHPA536Q32WR. The curl uses a placeholder 'https://BaseURL/PlaceOrder'.
- Request is form/query style: jData (the JSON order object) and jKey are joined with '&' in the body/query, not a pure JSON body.
- URL-encode trading symbols containing special characters (e.g. M&M) in tsym/tsym2/tsym3 to avoid errors.
- trgprc only to be sent for SL / SL-M orders.
- dscqty (disclosed qty) max is 10% of qty for NSE and 50% for MCX.
- prd values map: C=CNC, M=NRML, H=CO (Cover Order / High Leverage), B=BO (Bracket Order), I=MIS, F=MTF. Select valid prd from the 'prarr' array in User Details for the chosen exchange.
- exch must be chosen from the 'exarr' array in the User Details response.
- Bracket/Cover-order extras: bpprc (Book Profit) for product B; blprc (Book Loss) and trailprc (Trailing) for products H and B.
- amo: send "amo":"Yes" for after-market orders; if not an AMO, omit the field entirely (sending it wrong yields "Invalid AMO").
- Multi-leg legs (tsym2/trantype2/qty2/prc2 and tsym3/trantype3/qty3/prc3) are mandatory for price types 2L and 3L respectively.
- request_time in responses is a formatted timestamp string "HH:MM:SS DD-MM-YYYY" (e.g. 10:48:03 20-05-2020), not epoch.
- ordersource should be set to API to generate exchange info fields.
- The RESPONSE DETAILS table documents only stat, request_time and norenordno; emsg is returned on failure (see sample failure response) but is not listed in that table.
